title: Nexus examples index
description: Machine-readable index of the example corpus shipped under examples/. Consumed by `nexus examples <symbol>` (forthcoming) and by nexus-skill --emit for LLM training/reference. Two kinds of entry: 'positive' (compilable, executable) and 'negative' (intentionally broken; the diagnostic is the artifact).

version.description: Index schema major version. Bump on breaking entry-shape changes.

generated_at.description: ISO-8601 date the index was regenerated. Informational only.

kind.description: 'positive' = compiles + runs; 'negative' = deliberately broken, asserts a diagnostic.

summary.description: One-paragraph description of what the example demonstrates.

topics.description: Searchable tags: stdlib module names, language features (e.g. 'linear-types', 'handlers', 'caps', 'lazy-parallel').

stdlib.description: stdlib modules referenced (without the 'std:' prefix), e.g. ['stdio', 'list'].

require.description: Caps appearing in the file's `require { ... }` rows (set semantics — order not load-bearing).

throws.description: Exception groups / exceptions appearing in `throws { ... }` rows.

expect_fail.description: Negative-kind only. The diagnostic code the runner asserts (e.g. 'E2007').

expect_msg.description: Negative-kind only. Substrings the runner additionally asserts inside the diagnostic body.
